page: Microsoft Azure D4ads_v6 is a general-purpose virtual machine belonging to the Dadv6 server family. Powered by an AMD EPYC 9V74 processor utilizing the x86_64 architecture, this instance features 4 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 2 physical cores with multithreading enabled. It is equipped with 16.0 GB of system memory, yielding 4.0 GB per core, and includes 236 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability. The underlying virtualization is managed via Microsoft Hyper-V.

Performance benchmarks reveal strong single-core capabilities, with stress-ng single-core CPU and Geekbench single-core scores placing in the top 25 percent. However, multi-core performance and memory bandwidth present some limitations. While multi-core stress-ng and PassMark CPU scores sit in the average range, memory read bandwidth is weak, measuring 303.66 GB/sec for cached small blocks and 37.93 GB/sec for uncached large blocks. Cryptographic operations and integer math perform adequately, supported by hardware-accelerated instructions like AES, VAES, and SHA_NI.

This hardware profile offers a balanced resource density suitable for general-purpose workloads, web serving, and lightweight database operations. Due to weak multi-threaded decompression and lower memory read bandwidth, it is less suited for heavy compilation, ray tracing, or large-scale LLM inference, where prompt processing for a 7B model drops to a poor 11.76 tokens per second. It represents a cost-efficient option for applications requiring dedicated AMD EPYC execution and moderate local storage without the premium cost of memory-optimized or GPU-accelerated instances.
description: Microsoft Azure D4ads_v6 is a general-purpose virtual machine from the Dadv6 family, featuring 4 dedicated vCPUs and 16.0 GB of RAM powered by an AMD EPYC 9V74 processor at 3.7 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture, it includes 236 GB of local storage and is Premium Storage capable. Benchmarks indicate strong single-core CPU and text processing performance, though memory read bandwidth and multi-threaded decompression are relatively weak. With its balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio and dedicated CPU allocation, this instance is well-suited for general-purpose applications, static web serving, and moderate Redis workloads, offering a resource-dense and cost-efficient profile for workloads that do not require massive memory arrays or GPU acceleration.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D4ads_v6 is a general-purpose VM featuring 4 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs, 16 GB RAM, and 236 GB local storage, delivering strong single-core performance for balanced workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D4ads_v6 is a general-purpose VM with 4 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 16 GB RAM, and 236 GB local storage, managed via Microsoft Hyper-V.
tagline: A balanced x86_64 virtual machine featuring dedicated AMD EPYC processing and local storage for general-purpose workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by an AMD EPYC 9V74 processor running at 3.7 GHz
Features 4 dedicated vCPUs, 16.0 GB RAM, and 236 GB local storage
Strong single-core CPU and text processing benchmark results
Equipped with hardware-accelerated cryptography and vector instructions
Best suited for general-purpose computing, static web serving, and Redis
